title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: working Julia arrays R via JuliaCall, every transfer copies data. 50,000 x 25,000 Float64 matrix (~9.3 GB), means allocating 9.3 GB R side spending seconds copy. iterating exploratory analysis building pipeline shuttles arrays back forth, copies add fast. jlview eliminates overhead using R’s ALTREP (Alternative Representations) framework. Instead copying, jlview() returns lightweight R vector whose data pointer points directly Julia’s memory. R operations like sum(), subsetting, colMeans() read Julia’s buffer zero additional allocation. Benchmark: 50K x 25K Float64 matrix (9.3 GB)

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Install jlview GitHub: using jlview, initialize Julia runtime via JuliaCall: julia_setup() call required per R session. jlview automatically load Julia-side support module first call jlview().
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tanaylab/jlview") library(jlview) JuliaCall::julia_setup()

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Create Julia vector wrap ALTREP view:
code: JuliaCall::julia_command("v = randn(100_000)") x <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("v"))  length(x) # 100000 sum(x) # computed directly from Julia memory x[1:5] # subsetting works as usual

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Two-dimensional Julia arrays become R matrices proper dimensions:
code: JuliaCall::julia_command("M = randn(1000, 500)") m <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("M"))  dim(m) # [1] 1000  500 m[1:3, 1:3] # subset rows and columns colSums(m) # column sums, no copy

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: can confirm R-side allocation occurred checking .altrep():
code: .Internal(inspect(x)) # Should show ALTREP wrapper, not a materialized REALSXP

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: jlview supports following Julia element types: conversion strategy deliberate. Types like Float32 Int64 direct R counterpart matching memory layout. jlview converts Julia side layout-compatible type (Float64 Int32), pins converted array, creates zero-copy view . one-time conversion cost small compared copying across runtimes. Bool String arrays, memory layouts fundamentally incompatible (Julia Bool 1 byte, R logical 4 bytes; Julia strings GC-managed objects). fall back JuliaCall’s standard copy path, jlview() emit warning.

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Julia’s NamedArrays package provides named dimensions. jlview dedicated functions preserve names without triggering ALTREP materialization.

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
code: JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command('nv = NamedArray([10.0, 20.0, 30.0], (["a", "b", "c"],))') x <- jlview_named_vector(JuliaCall::julia_eval("nv"))  names(x) # [1] "a" "b" "c" x["b"] # 20, still zero-copy for the data

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Names attached atomically ALTREP construction. important setting names() dimnames() existing ALTREP vector normally trigger materialization (full copy), defeating purpose. passing names jlview(..., names = ...) jlview(..., dimnames = ...), names set ALTREP object R ever inspects data.
code: JuliaCall::julia_command('nm = NamedArray(randn(3, 2), (["r1","r2","r3"], ["c1","c2"]))') m <- jlview_named_matrix(JuliaCall::julia_eval("nm"))  rownames(m) # [1] "r1" "r2" "r3" colnames(m) # [1] "c1" "c2" m["r1", "c2"]

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Julia’s SparseMatrixCSC maps naturally R’s dgCMatrix Matrix package. jlview_sparse() constructs dgCMatrix nonzero values (x slot) backed zero-copy ALTREP view Julia’s nzval array. row indices (slot) column pointers (p slot) require 1--0 index shift (Julia 1-based, dgCMatrix 0-based). copied shifted Julia returned R plain integer vectors.
code: JuliaCall::julia_command("using SparseArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command("sp = sprand(Float64, 10000, 5000, 0.01)") s <- jlview_sparse(JuliaCall::julia_eval("sp"))  class(s) # [1] "dgCMatrix" dim(s) # [1] 10000  5000 Matrix::nnzero(s)

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: jlview pins Julia arrays global dictionary prevent Julia’s garbage collector reclaiming R holds reference. means Julia memory held long R ALTREP object exists.

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: Pinning dictionary – array stored JlviewSupport.PINNED unique ID. C finalizer R ALTREP object calls unpin() R garbage-collects wrapper. GC pressure tracking – jlview tracks total pinned bytes reports R via R_AdjustExternalMemory(). pinned memory exceeds threshold (default 10 GB), jlview forces R gc() reclaim stale ALTREP objects. Explicit release – tight control, call jlview_release() immediately unpin array without waiting R’s GC.

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
code: m <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("randn(10000, 1000)")) # ... use m ... jlview_release(m) # m is now invalid; accessing it will error

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: with_jlview() guarantees release even error occurs:
code: result <- with_jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("randn(100000)"), {     c(mean(.x), sd(.x)) }) # .x is automatically released here, result is a plain R vector

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
code: # Check current state jlview_gc_pressure() # $pinned_bytes # [1] 80000000 # $threshold # [1] 10737418240  # Lower the threshold to 500 MB jlview_set_gc_threshold(500e6)

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: jlview objects follow R’s standard copy--write (COW) semantics. Read operations (subsetting, aggregation, printing) zero-copy. Write operations trigger materialization: R allocates fresh buffer, copies data Julia, ALTREP wrapper replaced standard R vector. identical R treats shared vector – jlview introduce new semantics. difference materialization, backing store Julia memory instead R memory.
code: x <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("collect(1.0:5.0)")) y <- x # y and x share Julia memory, no copy sum(y) # zero-copy read  y[1] <- 999.0 # WRITE: triggers materialization # y is now a standard R numeric vector (copy of Julia data, modified) # x still points to Julia memory, unchanged

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: jlview objects can saved saveRDS() restored readRDS(). save, data materialized standard R vector (since Julia memory serialized). load, get back regular R vector. means serialization always works correctly, zero-copy property preserved across save/load cycles.
code: x <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("randn(1000)")) saveRDS(x, "my_vector.rds")  # In a new session (no Julia needed): y <- readRDS("my_vector.rds") class(y) # "numeric" -- a plain R vector

title: Zero-Copy Julia Arrays in R with jlview
text: NA_integer_ collision – R uses INT_MIN (-2147483648) represent NA_integer_. Julia Int32 array contains exact value, R interpret NA. workaround short avoiding sentinel value Julia integer arrays. Int64 precision loss – Julia Int64 values outside range +/-(2^53 - 1) lose precision converted Float64. jlview emits warning detected, conversion still proceeds. Bool String always copy – Julia’s Bool (1 byte) incompatible R’s logical (4 bytes), Julia strings GC-managed objects contiguous memory layout R can point . types always fall back full copy via JuliaCall. Write-back supported – Modifications jlview objects propagate back Julia. Writes trigger R’s copy--write, producing independent R vector. Single-session lifetime – jlview objects tied Julia runtime current R session. shared across processes serialized without materialization.

what: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: jlview provides zero-copy R views Julia-owned arrays using R’s ALTREP (Alternative Representations) framework. Instead copying data Julia R, jlview returns lightweight R vectors point directly Julia’s memory. Benchmark: 50K × 25K Float64 matrix (9.3 GB)

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: can install package CRAN: can install package GitHub:
code: install.packages("jlview") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tanaylab/jlview")

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: R ≥ 4.0 Julia ≥ 1.6 JuliaCall R package

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
code: library(jlview) JuliaCall::julia_setup()  # Create a Julia array JuliaCall::julia_command("x = randn(10000, 1000)")  # Zero-copy view — R sees Julia's memory directly m <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("x")) dim(m) # [1] 10000  1000 sum(m) # works natively, no data copied  # Named arrays are supported JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command("named = NamedArray(randn(3), [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])") v <- jlview_named_vector(JuliaCall::julia_eval("named")) v["a"] # access by name, still zero-copy  # Sparse matrices (zero-copy values, shifted indices) JuliaCall::julia_command("using SparseArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command("sp = sprand(1000, 500, 0.01)") s <- jlview_sparse(JuliaCall::julia_eval("sp")) class(s) # "dgCMatrix"  # Explicit release to free Julia memory early jlview_release(m)

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: Zero-copy dense arrays — Float64, Int32 map directly R’s REALSXP, INTSXP Type conversion — Float32, Int64, Int16 converted Julia, zero-copy R Named arrays — NamedArray row/column names attached atomically without triggering copy Sparse matrices — dgCMatrix zero-copy values (nzval) shifted indices Copy--write — R’s standard COW semantics: reads zero-copy, writes trigger materialization GC safety — Julia arrays pinned R holds references; three-layer defense (pinning dict, memory pressure tracking, explicit release) Fork safety — Safe parallel::mclapply() (PID-guarded finalizers) Serialization — saveRDS()/readRDS() work correctly (materializes save)

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: jlview uses R’s ALTREP framework create R vectors backed Julia memory: Pin — Julia array stored global dictionary, preventing Julia’s GC collecting Wrap — ALTREP R vector created whose Dataptr returns Julia’s raw data pointer Use — R operations (sum, subsetting, etc.) read directly Julia’s memory Release — R garbage-collects ALTREP object, C finalizer calls Julia unpin array entire pin→ALTREP→finalizer path implemented C using Julia’s C API (jl_call1), avoiding JuliaCall overhead ensuring safety R’s garbage collection.

what: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: Check object jlview ALTREP vector

title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
code: is_jlview(x)

title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: x R object

title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: TRUE x jlview ALTREP vector, FALSE otherwise

what: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: Creates ALTREP vector (matrix, 2D+) backed Julia memory. resulting R object shares memory Julia array, avoiding data copying. Modifications R object trigger copy--write (unless writeable = TRUE).

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
code: jlview(julia_array, writeable = FALSE, names = NULL, dimnames = NULL)

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: julia_array JuliaObject referencing Julia array writeable TRUE, allow R write directly Julia's memory. Use caution — enables shared mutation. Default FALSE. names Optional character vector names attach result. Attached atomically construction avoid ALTREP materialization. dimnames Optional list dimnames attach result. Attached atomically construction avoid ALTREP materialization.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: ALTREP vector backed Julia memory, standard R vector Julia type supported zero-copy.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ JuliaCall::julia_setup() # Create a Julia array and view it in R without copying JuliaCall::julia_command("x = randn(1000)") x <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("x")) sum(x) # operates directly on Julia memory } # }

title: Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure

title: Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure
code: jlview_gc_pressure()

title: Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure
text: list pinned_bytes threshold

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
text: Creates zero-copy ALTREP view Julia NamedArray matrix, preserving row column names dimnames.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix

text: ALTREP matrix dimnames set Julia NamedArray

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ JuliaCall::julia_setup() JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays") m <- JuliaCall::julia_eval('NamedArray(randn(3,2), (["a","b","c"], ["x","y"]))') x <- jlview_named_matrix(m) rownames(x) # returns c("a", "b", "c") colnames(x) # returns c("x", "y") } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ JuliaCall::julia_setup() JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays") v <- JuliaCall::julia_eval('NamedArray([1.0, 2.0, 3.0], (["a", "b", "c"],))') x <- jlview_named_vector(v) names(x) # returns c("a", "b", "c") } # }

text: total pinned bytes exceeds threshold, jlview forces R garbage collection reclaim stale ALTREP objects. Default 10GB.

text: bytes Threshold bytes (numeric)

title: Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold

what: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: Creates dgCMatrix-class backed zero-copy ALTREP vector nonzero values (x slot). row indices (slot) column pointers (p slot) copied shifted 1-based (Julia) 0-based (R) indexing Julia, returned plain R integer vectors.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
code: jlview_sparse(julia_sparse_matrix, lazy_indices = FALSE)

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: julia_sparse_matrix JuliaObject referencing Julia SparseMatrixCSC. value type must supported zero-copy (Float64, Float32, Int32, Int64, Int16, UInt8). lazy_indices Ignored. Retained API compatibility . Previously controlled lazy vs eager materialization ALTREP index vectors, removed favor simple copy+shift Julia.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: dgCMatrix-class sparse matrix.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ JuliaCall::julia_setup() JuliaCall::julia_command("using SparseArrays") m <- JuliaCall::julia_eval("sprand(Float64, 100, 50, 0.1)") s <- jlview_sparse(m) class(s) # "dgCMatrix" } # }

what: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: Creates jlview object ensures released scope exits, even error occurs. prevents memory leaks forgotten releases.

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
code: with_jlview(   julia_array,   expr,   writeable = FALSE,   names = NULL,   dimnames = NULL )

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: julia_array JuliaObject referencing Julia array expr expression evaluate jlview object bound .x writeable Passed jlview names Passed jlview dimnames Passed jlview

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: result evaluating expr

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ JuliaCall::julia_setup() JuliaCall::julia_command("big = randn(100000)") result <- with_jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("big"), {     c(mean(.x), sd(.x)) }) # .x is automatically released here } # }

title: jlview 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-23 Initial release.

title: jlview 0.1.0
text: Zero-copy R views Julia Float64 Int32 arrays via ALTREP Automatic type conversion Float32, Int64, Int16, UInt8 arrays Sparse matrix support (dgCMatrix) zero-copy nonzero values Named vector matrix support atomic dimnames (COW) Scope-based release with_jlview() ALTREP Sum/Min/Max methods calling Julia directly performance GC pressure tracking configurable threshold Safe cross-runtime garbage collection via C-level finalizers Fork safety (mclapply), Julia shutdown safety, double-release protection Full serialization support (saveRDS/readRDS)
